title: Some rules to fill NaNs
author: Vincent Debout
body_markdown:
I denote "Children", persons with age <= 12 years old.

Here are some rules I identified to fill NaNs:

- link between HomePlanet and CabinDeck:
    Earth on E, F,G
    Mars on D, E, F
    Europa on A, B, C, D, E, T
- persons in the same group all have the same CabinSide, possibly on different CabinDeck
- in groups with several CabinDeck, people with no bill are necessarily on CabinDeck:
   Earth on G
   Europa on B
   Mars on E or F
- for Europa groups with several CabinDeck, people with no bill are on deck B
- CabinNum are in increasing order of PassengerIdGroup for each (CabinDeck, CabinSide)
  => This is very useful but need a complex algorithm. All Cabins are occupied ! 
- People in a group have the same HomePlanet
- Surnames are found in a unique HomePlanet
- Children have no bill
- CryoSleep have no bill
- People not Children and not CryoSleep with no bill all have Destination TRAPPIST-1e
- link between HomePlanet, VIP (also Age and CryoSleep):
   Earth no VIP
   Mars VIP have Age >= 18 and no CryoSleep and never goes to "55 Cancri e"
   Europa VIP have Age >= 25
- Europa with Age < 25 never goes to "PSO J318.5-22"
- Mars children are all transported

Approximative rules (with some exceptions):
- Firstnames are found in a unique HomePlanet
- ToTal bill has minimum per HomePlanet and CabinDeck (Earth is around 300, Mars 400 and Europa 1000), be careful when filling with median


Please let me know if you identify additionnal rules

title: A Summary of all top discussion posts
author: The Devastator
body_markdown:
## A Summary of all top discussion posts
Below is a summary of all the top discussion posts of this competition so far. 
Made by myself. 
_____


[Make the best use of "Deck" feature](https://www.kaggle.com/competitions/spaceship-titanic/discussion/310238) 
by [mostafaalaa123](mostafaalaa123)

- From the comments:
- I think the idea is to create a new feature that is 1 if the deck is safe and 0 if it is dangerous.

["Gender" didn't help me](https://www.kaggle.com/competitions/spaceship-titanic/discussion/310239) 
by [mostafaalaa123](mostafaalaa123)

- Adding gender as a feature didn't help the author's model.

[Spaceship Titanic Compare BaseModels EveryBody Get Insight!](https://www.kaggle.com/competitions/spaceship-titanic/discussion/309535)
by [kalelpark](kalelpark)

The author used pycaret to compere many models to try and find the best one. These were the results:

![](https://i.ibb.co/26xKhJd/2022-02-24-094651.jpg)

**Top 5 Models**
- LightGBM - 79.1%
- GradientBoostingClassifier - 79.6%
- AdaBoostClassifier - 79.4%
- RandomForest - 79.2%
- CatBoost - 79.6%

[Question - How to deal with this kind of distribution ?](https://www.kaggle.com/competitions/spaceship-titanic/discussion/310009) 
by [kartik2khandelwal](kartik2khandelwal)

- The author was looking at the distribution of some of the features and asked for guidance about dealing with such a distribution.

**Options proposed**
- **Remove Outliers** - but this will result in less data
- **Perform some transformation technique** - log, sqrt, boxcox and so on…
- **Make it a categorical variable** by putting all the outliers as 0 and other data as 1 - may leads to less information.

**From the comments**

- **Remove Outliers** - I would advise not to do so. Usually, if we remove outliers it is because we make the hypothesis that the event is due to some kind of artifact (a wrong record). But here, people paying a lot is not a wrong record and it could actually be valuable data.
- **Perform some transformation** - Every variable is not meant to be normally distributed. I think we can even expect this variable to be distributed as shown as we would expect only a few amounts of people to be able to spend that much. If you use log transform, for example, you will only compress your data and lose most of the value it holds. Except if you need to approximate a normal distribution for analysis purposes, or because you need to satisfy the normal distribution hypothesis for one of your processing steps, I would suggest not to.
- **Make it a categorical variable** - As discussed previously, we can't really talk of outliers here. An outlier is a record that seems abnormal based on the hypothesis of the distribution it follows. So, I would suggest not applying this categorical transformation either.

- **Perform some transformation** One could use a non-parametric estimator, such as decision trees or developments thereof, such as RandomForest, XGBoost, or HistGradientBoostingClassifier etc. thus obviating the need to transform the features to become Gaussian.
- **Make it a categorical variable** Rather than actually replacing the feature as a categorical variable, create a new additional feature as a categorical variable, perhaps with something like

- `df['food_spending'] = np.where(df['FoodCourt'] > 1000, 'yes', 'no')`


[[Exploratory Data Analysis] High cardinality & High correlation](https://www.kaggle.com/competitions/spaceship-titanic/discussion/309513) 
by [rishirajacharya](rishirajacharya)

The author is sharing some findings of EDA:

- `PassengerId` has a high cardinality: 8693 distinct values
- `Cabin` has a high cardinality: 6560 distinct values
- `Name` has a high cardinality: 8473 distinct values
- `FoodCourt` is highly correlated with VRDeck`
- `CryoSleep` is highly correlated with `Transported`
- `HomePlanet` is highly correlated with `Destination`

[Tip - Don't forget about the Cabin! 📈](https://www.kaggle.com/competitions/spaceship-titanic/discussion/309693)
by [alexteboul](alexteboul)

- From the post: "For a slight **increase in accuracy**, don't drop the `Cabin` column entirely from your modeling."

**For Example**
```python
# split column and add new columns to df
train[['Deck', 'Num', 'Side']] = train['Cabin'].str.split('/', expand=True)
# display the dataframe
train.head()
```
- [notebook](https://www.kaggle.com/alexteboul/space-titanic-eda-models-lb-0-799l)

[How to Choose OneHotEncoding between LabelEncoding? I'll Answer!! (EDA Tip)](https://www.kaggle.com/competitions/spaceship-titanic/discussion/309803) 
by [kalelpark](kalelpark)

- From the post:

**We apply One-Hot Encoding when:**

- The Categorical feature is not ordinal
- The number of categorical features is less so one-hot encoding can be effectively applied

**We apply Label Encoding when:**
- The categorical feature is ordinal (like Primary school, high school)
- The number of categories is quite large as one-hot encoding can lead to high memory consumption

The author also shared a public [notebook](https://www.kaggle.com/kalelpark/spaceship-titanic-solutions-for-beginner)

[Might be a good way to fill some missing  value?](https://www.kaggle.com/competitions/spaceship-titanic/discussion/310574) 
by [gargantuar](gargantuar)

- Found that for the passenger when the first part of the PassengerId is same ,the Homeplanet ,Cabin are also same.
- So, we can fill some missing value of these two values(about 50 in total 200),that might be able to improve the data quality.

[Adding GENDER Feature based on NAME column](https://www.kaggle.com/competitions/spaceship-titanic/discussion/309664) 
by [sardorabdirayimov](sardorabdirayimov)

- The notebook uses an [external web page](https://www.gpeters.com/names/baby-names.php) to identify gender based on the name
- Also: `Name` column in the dataset definitely helps to identify `gender`, `family`, `nationality` , `personality`


[📌 EDA📈 + FE🔥 + Comparison⚖️ + Pseudo🎭](https://www.kaggle.com/competitions/spaceship-titanic/discussion/309626) 
by [hasanbasriakcay](hasanbasriakcay)

- **Shared multiple notebooks:**
- [EDA](https://www.kaggle.com/hasanbasriakcay/spaceship-titanic-eda-fe-baseline)
- [Model Comparison](https://www.kaggle.com/hasanbasriakcay/spaceship-titanic-pycaret-model-comparisons)
- [Pseudo Labeling](https://www.kaggle.com/hasanbasriakcay/spaceship-titanic-pseudo-labels-stacking)

[Cabin Feature is more IMPORTANT than you believe](https://www.kaggle.com/competitions/spaceship-titanic/discussion/319152)
by [javigallego](javigallego)

- Sharing a code snippet for splitting the Cabin feature into 3 new features.
```python
def split_Cabin(df):
    df['Deck'] = df['Cabin'].str.split("/", n=2, expand=True)[0]
    df['Number'] = df['Cabin'].str.split("/", n=2, expand=True)[1]
    df['Side'] = df['Cabin'].str.split("/", n=2, expand=True)[2]
    df.pop('Cabin')
    return df
```

[Feature engineering results](https://www.kaggle.com/competitions/spaceship-titanic/discussion/316768)
by [samuelcortinhas](samuelcortinhas)

**Sharing the results of Feature Engineering:**
- drop VIP feature - 0.02% increase
- create an expenditure feature that sums the total money spent across the 5 amenities - 0.5% increase
- create expenditure feature and drop the 5 amenities (RoomService, …,VRDeck) - 4.7% decrease
- create a cabin deck feature (A,B,…,T) - 0.35% increase
- create a cabin side feature (P/S) - 0.9% increase
- create a group size feature from the PassengerId column - 0.1% increase
- create a no spending binary feature that is 1 if no money was spent and 0 otherwise - 0.2% increase
- Create a solo feature that tracks if passenger was travelling on their own (from PassengerId) - 0.2% increase
- Create cabin region features according to chunks of 300 passengers (see here for details) - 0.2% increase-
- create a family size feature from last name - 0.0% change
- create binary age features for 0-18 yo, 18-25 yo, 25+ yo - 0.02% increase
- same as above but drop the age column - 0.4% drop

**Conclusions:**
- create total expenditure, cabin deck and cabin side features - these are very helpful
- group size, solo, no_spending and cabin regions also help a bit

[full notebook here](https://www.kaggle.com/code/samuelcortinhas/spaceship-titanic-a-complete-guide)

[Cabin number chunking](https://www.kaggle.com/competitions/spaceship-titanic/discussion/316379) 
by [samuelcortinhas](samuelcortinhas)

- From the post: "It seems that the cabin number is chunked into groups of 300 cabins, and within each group the target distribution is a little bit different."
![](https://i.postimg.cc/NjpRsTXT/Cabin-number-distribution.png)


[Speed up your kernel using Intel® Extension for Scikit-learn](https://www.kaggle.com/competitions/spaceship-titanic/discussion/314402) 
by [lordozvlad](lordozvlad)

-From the post: "If you want to speed up your kernel you can use Intel® Extension for Scikit-learn!"
```python
!pip install scikit-learn-intelex
```
- [notebook:  Introduction to scikit-learn-intelex](https://www.kaggle.com/lordozvlad/introduction-to-scikit-learn-intelex/notebook)

[🚀 Spaceship Titanic 0.79962 🚀](https://www.kaggle.com/competitions/spaceship-titanic/discussion/312497) 
by [rocklen](rocklen)

- Sharing a [notebook](https://www.kaggle.com/code/rocklen/spaceship-titanic-2-0-80079) with a score of 0.79962 using XGBoost + Randomized Search

[Random Forest Perform significantly better in some cases than XGBoost](https://www.kaggle.com/competitions/spaceship-titanic/discussion/314044)
by [shivamnegi1993](shivamnegi1993)

- Random Forest is performing better than XGBoost in some cases.
- Check cases on the original thread.

[Some rules to fill NaNs](https://www.kaggle.com/competitions/spaceship-titanic/discussion/315987) 
by [vdebout](vdebout)

- The author shared some hardcoded rules to fill NaNs.

- link between HomePlanet and CabinDeck:
    Earth on E, F,G
    Mars on D, E, F
    Europa on A, B, C, D, E, T
- persons in the same group all have the same CabinSide, possibly on different CabinDeck
    in groups with several CabinDeck, people with no bill are necessarily on CabinDeck:
    Earth on G
    Europa on B
    Mars on E or F
- for Europa groups with several CabinDeck, people with no bill are on deck B
- CabinNum are in increasing order of PassengerIdGroup for each (CabinDeck, CabinSide) => This is very useful but need a complex algorithm. All Cabins are occupied !
- People in a group have the same HomePlanet
- Surnames are found in a unique HomePlanet
- Children have no bill
- CryoSleep have no bill
- People not Children and not CryoSleep with no bill all have Destination TRAPPIST-1e
- link between HomePlanet, VIP (also Age and CryoSleep):
    Earth no VIP
    Mars VIP have Age >= 18 and no CryoSleep and never goes to "55 Cancri e"
    Europa VIP have Age >= 25
    Europa with Age < 25 never goes to "PSO J318.5-22"
    Mars children are all transported

**Approximative rules (with some exceptions):**
- Firstnames are found in a unique HomePlanet
- ToTal bill has minimum per HomePlanet and CabinDeck (Earth is around 300, Mars 400 and Europa 1000), be careful when filling with median


[Tip. Increase your score!](https://www.kaggle.com/competitions/spaceship-titanic/discussion/312173) 
by [michael6141](michael6141)

- Sharing a tip to increase your score: "Try to average received probabilities on each validation split (list(probabilities)/len(probabilities)), predict_proba may help you. Averaging the model is a powerful tool to get a high validation score."

[[Exploratory Data Analysis] Missing Values](https://www.kaggle.com/competitions/spaceship-titanic/discussion/309514)

- Spa has 183 (2.1%) missing values
- VIP has 203 (2.3%) missing values

[[Spaceship Titanic] TensorFlow Extended - Towards ML Engineering ](https://www.kaggle.com/competitions/spaceship-titanic/discussion/328882)
by [usharengaraju](usharengaraju)

- Sharing in depth analysis of the dataset using TensorFlow.
- Sharing a [public notebook](https://www.kaggle.com/harshjain123/time-series-arima-sarima) about the following time series concepts:
